datasources.name: kubernetes_cluster_v2
datasources.description: Retrieves information about an existing next-generation Kubernetes cluster.

string.description: The region in which the cluster resides. If omitted, the provider's configured `region` is used.
The cluster's ID. Used to fetch a specific cluster.
The cluster's UUID.
The installed Kubernetes version (e.g., `v1.34.2`).

bool.description: Indicates whether a floating IP is assigned to the cluster's API endpoint.

string.description: The user-assigned name of the cluster.
A user-provided description of the cluster.
The ID of the VPC network where the cluster is deployed.
The ID of the subnet used by the cluster's nodes.

set.description: The availability zones in which the master nodes reside.

string.description: The cluster type: `standard` (single AZ) or `regional` (three AZs).
The ID of the external network providing internet access to the cluster.

set.description: A list of container registry addresses that can be accessed without certificate validation.

string.description: The CNI network plugin in use (always `calico`).
The IPv4 CIDR block allocated for pod networking.

map.description: Kubernetes labels applied to the entire cluster.

string.description: The ID of the subnet where LoadBalancer type services are provisioned.

set.description: CIDR blocks permitted to access the cluster's API load balancer.

int64.description: The number of master nodes (1, 3, or 5) ensuring etcd quorum.

string.description: The flavor ID (VM type) used for master nodes.

set_nested.description: Information about the disks attached to master nodes (root, etcd, etcd-events). Each object contains `type` (disk type) and `size` (GB).

string.description: The storage volume type used for this disk (e.g., `ceph-ssd`, `ceph-hdd`). Corresponds to the available volume types in the region.

int64.description: The allocated size of the disk in gigabytes (GB).

string.description: The raw kubeconfig file contents used to authenticate to the Kubernetes API.
The timestamp when the cluster was created (ISO 8601 format).
The current operational status of the cluster (e.g., `RUNNING`, `RECONCILING`).
The VK Cloud project ID owning the cluster.
The floating IP address of the API load balancer (present only when `public_ip` is `true`).
The internal virtual IP address of the API load balancer.
The full URL of the Kubernetes API server (e.g., `https://api.example.com:6443`).

set_nested.description: A list of node groups associated with the cluster. Each object includes `id`, `name`, `flavor`, `node_count`, and `availability_zone`.

string.description: The unique identifier of the node group.
The user-assigned name of the node group.
The flavor ID (VM type) used for each worker node in this group.

int64.description: The current number of worker nodes in the group. This value reflects the actual node count, accounting for both manual scaling and autoscaling actions.

string.description: The availability zone where the nodes of this group are deployed.

datasources.name: kubernetes_node_group_v2
datasources.description: Retrieves information about a specific node group within a next-generation Kubernetes cluster.

string.description: The region where the node group resides. Defaults to the provider's `region`.
The ID of the node group to retrieve.
The ID of the parent Kubernetes cluster.
The node group's UUID
The user-assigned name of the node group.
The flavor ID (VM type) of each node in the group.
The availability zone where the nodes are located.
The scaling strategy: `fixed_scale` (static node count) or `auto_scale` (dynamic based on load).

int64.description: The desired number of nodes when `scale_type` is `fixed_scale`.
The current number of nodes when `scale_type` is `auto_scale`.
The minimum allowed node count for `auto_scale`.
The maximum allowed node count for `auto_scale`.

map.description: Kubernetes labels applied to the nodes in this group.

set_nested.description: Taints applied to the nodes. Each taint contains `key`, `value`, and `effect` (e.g., `NoSchedule`).

string.description: The taint key. Must conform to Kubernetes label key syntax (e.g., `node.kubernetes.io/unreachable`).
The taint value. Must conform to Kubernetes label value syntax (e.g., `production`).
The effect of the taint. Allowed values are: `NoSchedule`, `PreferNoSchedule`, `NoExecute`.

int64.description: The maximum percentage of nodes that can be upgraded simultaneously (1-100).

string.description: The type of root volume attached to each node (e.g., `ceph-ssd`).

int64.description: The size of the root volume in GB.

string.description: The creation timestamp of the node group (ISO 8601 format).

datasources.name: kubernetes_versions_v2
datasources.description: Returns a set of Kubernetes versions available for provisioning next-generation clusters.

string.description: The region for which to retrieve available versions. Defaults to provider's `region`.
A synthetic identifier set to "kubernetes_versions". This data source does not have a natural ID.

set.description: A set of available Kubernetes version strings (e.g., ["v1.33.3", "v1.34.2"]).

datasources.name: kubernetes_volume_types_v2
datasources.description: Returns a set of available volume types with their supported availability zones for node group disks.

string.description: The region for which to retrieve volume types. Defaults to provider's `region`.
A synthetic identifier set to "volume_types". This data source does not have a natural ID.

set_nested.description: A set of available volume types with their supported availability zones.

string.description: The name of the volume type (e.g., "ceph-hdd", "high-iops").

set.description: A set of availability zones where this volume type is available (e.g., ["PA2", "MS1", "ME1"]).

resources.name: kubernetes_cluster_v2
resources.description: Creates and manages a next-generation Kubernetes cluster.

single_nested.description: Timeouts configuration for create, update and delete operations.

string.description: Timeout for create operation (e.g., `60m`, `2h`). Default is `60m`.
Timeout for update operation (e.g., `120m`, `3h`). Default is `60m`.
Timeout for delete operation (e.g., `30m`, `1h`). Default is `30m`.
The region in which to create the cluster. If omitted, the provider's `region` is used. **Forces replacement** on change.
The unique identifier of the cluster.
The cluster UUID. Can be specified; if not, it is generated automatically. **Changing this forces a new cluster.**
The Kubernetes version to deploy. Only upgrades to a higher version are allowed.

bool.description: If `true`, a floating IP is assigned to the API endpoint. `Forces replacement` on change. Default is `false`.

string.description: A unique cluster name. Must be 3-25 lowercase alphanumeric characters or hyphens, start/end with alphanumeric, and contain no consecutive hyphens. Forces replacement on change.
A human-readable description of the cluster. **Forces replacement** on change. The maximum length is 256 characters.
The ID of the VPC network where the cluster will be deployed. The network must use the `sprut` SDN. **Forces replacement** on change.
The ID of the subnet for cluster nodes. **Forces replacement** on change.

set.description: A set of availability zones for the cluster's masters. For a `standard` cluster, provide 1, 3, or 5 zones. For a `regional` cluster, provide 3 or 5 zones. **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: Either `standard` (single AZ) or `regional` (three AZs). **Forces replacement** on change.
The ID of the external network for internet access. If omitted, the system selects one automatically. **Forces replacement** on change.

set.description: A set of registry addresses (e.g., `myregistry.com`) that can be accessed without TLS verification. **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: The CNI plugin to use. Currently only `calico` is supported. **Forces replacement** on change.
The IPv4 CIDR block for the pod network. Must be a valid private CIDR (e.g., `10.100.0.0/16`). **Forces replacement** on change.

map.description: A map of Kubernetes labels to apply to the cluster. Keys and values must conform to Kubernetes label syntax. **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: The ID of the subnet where LoadBalancer services will be allocated IPs. **Forces replacement** on change.

set.description: A set of CIDR blocks permitted to access the API load balancer. If empty, all IPs are allowed. **Forces replacement** on change.

int64.description: The number of master nodes. Must be an odd number to maintain etcd quorum (1, 3, or 5). **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: The flavor ID for master nodes. Changing this triggers a rolling upgrade of the control plane.

set_nested.description: Information about the master node disks. Each disk includes `type` and `size`.

string.description: The storage volume type provisioned for this master disk component.

int64.description: The size of the disk in GB.

string.description: The kubeconfig file contents. **Sensitive** — store securely.
The timestamp when the cluster was created (ISO 8601 format).
The current cluster status (e.g., `RUNNING`, `RECONCILING`).
The project ID that owns the cluster.
The floating IP of the API load balancer (present if `public_ip` = `true`).
The internal VIP of the API load balancer.
The URL of the Kubernetes API server.

set_nested.description: A list of node groups associated with the cluster. Each object contains `id`, `name`, `flavor`, `node_count`, and `availability_zone`.

string.description: The ID of the node group.
The name of the node group.
The flavor ID used for the worker nodes.

int64.description: The current number of nodes in the group.

string.description: The AZ where the node group resides.

resources.name: kubernetes_node_group_v2
resources.description: Creates and manages a node group within an existing next-generation Kubernetes cluster.

single_nested.description: Timeouts configuration for create, update and delete operations.

string.description: Timeout for create operation (e.g., `60m`, `2h`). Default is `30m`.
Timeout for update operation (e.g., `120m`, `3h`). Default is `30m`.
Timeout for delete operation (e.g., `30m`, `1h`). Default is `30m`.
The region where the node group will be created. Defaults to provider's `region`. **Forces replacement** on change.
The unique identifier of the node group.
The ID of the parent cluster. **Forces replacement** on change.
The node group's UUID. It is generated automatically.
A unique name for the node group within the cluster. Must be 3-25 characters, lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only. **Forces replacement** on change.
The flavor ID for each node. Changing this triggers a rolling upgrade of the nodes.
The availability zone where the nodes will be placed. **Forces replacement** on change.
Type of scaling for the node group. Must be either `fixed_scale` or `auto_scale`. If `scale_type` is `auto_scale`, the condition `auto_scale_min_size` <= `auto_scale_max_size` must be met.

int64.description: The desired number of nodes. Minimum value is 0. Required if `scale_type` is `fixed_scale`.
During the cluster lifecycle, it indicates the current number of nodes in the node group if `scale_type` is `auto_scale`.
The minimum number of nodes the autoscaler may scale down to. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Required if `scale_type` is `auto_scale`.
The maximum number of nodes the autoscaler may scale out to. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and also greater than or equal to `auto_scale_min_size`. Required if scale_type is `auto_scale`.

map.description: Kubernetes labels to apply to nodes in this group. Keys/values must be valid Kubernetes label strings.

set_nested.description: Taints to apply to nodes. Each taint must have `key`, `value`, and `effect` (one of `NoSchedule`, `PreferNoSchedule`, `NoExecute`).

string.description: The taint key. Must be a valid Kubernetes label key.
The taint value. Must be a valid Kubernetes label value.
The effect of the taint. Must be one of: `NoSchedule`, `PreferNoSchedule`, `NoExecute`.

int64.description: The maximum percentage of nodes (1-100) that can be upgraded simultaneously during a rolling upgrade.

string.description: The type of root volume (e.g., `ceph-ssd`). Use `vkcs_kubernetes_volume_types_v2` to list available types. **Forces replacement** on change.

int64.description: The size of the root volume in GB. Minimum is 1 GB. **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: The timestamp when the node group was created (ISO 8601 format).
